heading: Abigail Honn
lead: I love to sit still, but my mind never will. Understanding neural entrainment — and the way sound can quiet the mind — was revolutionary for me.
paragraphs: My introduction to sound meditation began while leading guided backpacking retreats in the mountains, where I’ve always felt how easily nature, stillness, and intentional rest could affect both the physical and emotional state. Through completing my degree in biology & ecology, I developed a deep appreciation for the way living systems naturally seek balance, restoration, and healing conditions.

Following individual mentorship and training in trauma-informed sound facilitation and nervous system regulation, I now offer sound-based experiences designed to support individuals through seasons of overwhelm, depletion, and healing — which, ultimately, includes us all.
